name: The castle of Aulencia — circular from Villanueva de la Cañada
name_original: El castillo de Aulencia — circular desde Villanueva de la Cañada
region_intro: Hiking around Villanueva De La Cañada offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by the Parque Regional Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama. This area features holm oak forests, groves, and riverside vegetation along the Guadarrama River. A well-developed trail network provides access to these natural features, including paths near the Aulencia Castle. Best hiking trails around Villanueva De La Cañada The most popular hiking route is The castle of Aulencia — circular from Villanueva de la Cañada, a 6.2 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around the historic Aulencia Castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Riparian forest loop from Ctra. M513 - Urb. Raya del Palancar, an easy 3.7 miles (6.0 km) path. This trail leads through riparian forest, offering views of the local flora. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Villanueva de la Cañada, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through varied local terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes. Hiking around Villanueva De La Cañada is defined by paths along the Guadarrama River, through holm oak forests, and near historical landmarks like Aulencia Castle. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 19 easy and 22 moderate trails available. The routes in Villanueva De La Cañada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De La Cañada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: Villanueva De La Cañada offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking trails. These routes cater to various preferences, with 19 easy and 22 moderate trails to explore.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: The hiking trails in Villanueva De La Cañada primarily traverse the &lt;strong&gt;Parque Regional Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama&lt;/strong&gt;. This protected area features rich flora, including extensive holm oak forests, riverside vegetation along the Guadarrama River, and various groves. You'll experience a blend of natural beauty and ecological diversity.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Villanueva De La Cañada has 19 easy trails, perfect for beginners or families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21955189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riparian forest loop from Ctra. M513 - Urb. Raya del Palancar&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.0 km path that takes about 1 hour 32 minutes to complete, offering pleasant views through the riparian forest.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: Many routes in the area are circular. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1378656126/the-castle-of-aulencia-circular-from-villanueva-de-la-canada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The castle of Aulencia — circular from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.1 km loop that explores the surroundings of the historic Aulencia Castle.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hikers can discover several notable landmarks. The prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1629985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aulencia Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a key attraction, often incorporated into routes. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1575300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Retamar Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3020485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Invincible Holm Oak&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Guadarrama River?
A: Yes, several trails offer paths along the Guadarrama River, allowing you to enjoy the riverside vegetation and serene environment. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21956499" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ford Over the Aulencia River – Guadarrama Riverbank loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.05 km route that includes sections along the riverbank.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on hikes in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: The elevation gain varies by trail difficulty. Easy routes typically have minimal elevation, while moderate trails can have up to 100-110 meters of ascent. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1378656126/the-castle-of-aulencia-circular-from-villanueva-de-la-canada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The castle of Aulencia — circular from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of about 110 meters.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: The routes in Villanueva De La Cañada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the natural beauty of the Guadarrama River area, and the well-maintained paths that lead to historical landmarks.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in the area?
A: While there are no trails classified as 'difficult' based on the SAC scale (exceeding 5 hours or 900 meters of elevation), there are 22 moderate trails that offer a good challenge. These routes require good physical condition and steady footing, such as the 14 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21956499" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ford Over the Aulencia River – Guadarrama Riverbank loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in the Parque Regional Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama?
A: The Parque Regional Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama is known for its ecological value and diverse fauna. You might spot various bird species, including the Imperial Eagle and black stork, as well as mammals like the wildcat, making it an interesting area for nature observation.

Q: Are there any trails that are particularly good for a shorter walk?
A: For a shorter walk, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21955119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt;. This easy 6.0 km trail can be completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes, offering a pleasant experience through varied local terrain.
tips:
The river Aulencia has been well crossed since the precarious wooden bridge was replaced by concrete cairns.
Almost all year round you can pedal on your bike, but you have to pay attention when doing it.
The castle is private property. Do not stop reading the owner's posters, they have no waste :-D
If the Aulencia River brings enough water, you will have to cross to the other side through the cement blocks on the side.
